author: garyrong
content:
> We are not checking any accounts until we are in the child frame, so consider the case where an account already exists at the address but you OOG the parent frame where previously your child frame just returned 0 in the case of a collision check, not sure about the existance of these kind of cases on chain.

I don't understand the concern here:

Originally, 
- if the gas is sufficient to pay the Create intrinsic cost (32k), and address collision happens, child frame returns 0 and parent frame is not affected
- if the gas is insufficient to pay the Create intrinsic cost (32k), parent is OOG

The behavior is not changed in 8037 right?

author: lu_pinto
content: conceptually did not change but the cost did, and that changes behaviour. Cost to OOG parent is 32k right now but it's going to be 183k + 9k after glamsterdam, so the OOG happens way easily even though there could be cases where you wouldn't OOG right now

author: garyrong
content: Yes it's true. But the root cause is we raise the state cost significantly, not the regular/state separation.

author: garyrong
content: The concern can be applied generally, like the state cost is raised so it may break some on-chain contracts

author: lu_pinto
content:
yes you could argue we are already raising the cost for writes anyway. I'm just on the cautious side since this does not OOG the current frame because things got expensive, but it OOGs the parent, rather than the child (current frame), which is gracefully handled by returning 0 in cases where it OOGs.
But anyway no strong feelings as before, if everyone feels like this is fine I'm ok with that.

author: spencer_tb
content:
EELS specs change for this: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/3002
Feel free to take a look!
